item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP itself is really good.
But i still have to downvote:
This is not Wondertainment

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The first image used in this article couldn't be sourced, and as such is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really lost a lot after the removal of the second picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I don't believe that this should be a Factory item, I do agree that this is not a Wondertainment-esque item. Also, this might just be me, but that guy in the image would most likely look a lot more scared and confused. I'd freak the fuck out if red foam started leaking out of my face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ignoring the whole 'danger' aspect of it, how would this entertain kids? I would be crying for momma if I started bleeding pink foam from eating bubblegum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote.

Its not fun, whimsical, or anything that is typically produced by Wondertainment. Not even a cutesy information block by Wondertainment that describes the product!

The associated GOI needs to change before I can upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote. I don't see this as fun or pseudo-educational. It doesn't fit what I understand to be Wondertainment's theme. Then again, neither do the "misters" to me. So what do I know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I happen to think of this more as an AWCY? Item

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came here to the comments section to…well, read the comments, of course, as I always do, but I came specifically to say "Factory, not Wondertainment". I see that's already a heated debate here, so I'll flesh out my argument some (and hopefully be able to articulate it in what my therapist calls "neurotypicalese"):

This could work fine as a stand-alone article, without involving any of the GOIs at all. The picture and anomalous effect make a perfect article on their own, even if you completely subtract any hints at the candy's source or origin. Not everything novelty has to be Factory or Wondertainment, or even MC&D for that matter.

If this must involve one of the GOIs, though, it should be the Factory as it stands now. I understand the argument that "pink with bubblegum scent" is a bit cutesy for the Factory, but as it is now, it's too morbid to be Wondertainment, considering it uses a subject's own blood to make the cutesy foam.

However, if it must be Wondertainment, what it needs is an over-the-top selling point label. Something that I've noticed about Dr. Wondertainment's products is that they have packaging labels that try to to advertise and sell them based on their fun, wondrous, and entertaining qualities - something that would make a person who sees it in a store pick it up, read it, think "Huh, that sounds cool", and then buy it. The warning label alone doesn't bring this scip to Wondertainment status, but I think a good addendum with a well-thought-out transcription of the packaging label easily could. I won't write such a thing myself, as I'm not up to SCP-publishing-par (in my opinion, at least), but I'm sure one of the old hands or someone who has done a Wondertainment article in the past could crank one out in their sleep.

Oh, and upvoted. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WHAT IS THE INTENDED FUNCTION OF THIS? WHO WOULD MAKE THIS FOR KIDS?

It's not a toy, it's a sweet that ruins your clothes, you can't even have a decent bath with it!

Nope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realised that I would never be able to eat only one of these things, it's actually pretty horrifying.

You've put me off sweets, you fucker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get the negative values until I read your comment. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I concur. Factory. There's no way to use this "safely". Wondertainment stuff should be safe if used properly (with a razor-thin margin of 'properly', of course).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing here that seems like a Wondertainment product is the color pink. This steals nutrients from people and makes them bleed, that's literally how it functions even under normal usage. I have never thought of 'leaking blood foam' and been reminded of Wondertainment.

"What shall we make today Johnny?"
"Candies that make you ooze foamy blood?"
"What the fuck are you talking about? How is that a kids toy at all?"
"Make it pink?"
"No Johnny, no… Why did I even hire you?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now this is the first SCP I'd like to "try out".

As for the problem how it should be classified, I am for Dr. Wondertainment: It looks innocent enough, and kid-friendly in the terms of appearance, which can't be said for a lot of Factory stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm… I think Wondertainment seems to fit with the theme of the SCP more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've tweaked this a tiny bit so that it is now made by the Factory as opposed to Dr. Wondertainment, if it starts getting downvotes I'll most likely swap it back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some pictures like it (and the picture itself) on the scp fuel photobucket account, upon originally writing this I had considered doing multiple flavours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It matched in an earlier draft, think I've fixed this now although I don't remember the original wording.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ummmmmm, blueberry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, blueberry is a possibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TAKE ONLY ONE!

Any other flavors on the market? Blueberry bile? Lavendar Lung? Strawberry cerebrospinal fliud?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture with the bubbles and the description of where they form doesn't match.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like I'm with the majority opinion here - this still feels more like a Factory item than a Wondertainment item for me, but I like it enough that I'm upvoting it. (The warning label addition saves it from being unforgivably out of character for Wondertainment, but still.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Attempts to trace these deliveries back to a distribution center or supplier have been unsuccessful as of ██/██/2005.

That pretty much says it all. We don't need every little detail of what was done trying to find the source, it's assumed the bases are covered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since its initial discovery in ██/██/1990, ██ deliveries have been made to various supermarkets globally. Attempts to trace these deliveries back to a distribution center or supplier have been unsuccessful as of ██/██/2005.

I think that the Foundation would investigate this a little bit more: did the shipped crates claim to be unsolicited promotional material, or did the supermarkets somehow attempt to order the gum? If they tried ordering it, how did they do so?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is fine just standalone without associating with either The Factory or Wondertainment. Why invite controversy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As do we all. See you in first class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's okay, I probably deserve to burn in hell for other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree for the most part, though The Factory DOES make toys as well as "practical" items. Like that "My First Anti-Gravity Belt" thing. Come to think of it, it seems The Factory makes more science-y or mystical stuff while Dr. Wondertainment makes more novelty items.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*throws a sheet over his conspiracy-theory string-board*
GET OUT OF MY APARTMENT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this, and reading through this discussion I'm on the fence about who should make it - The Factory or Dr. Wondertainment. But one comment caught me, which was Prof. Nemo's: both brands are equally lethal if used incorrectly (or correctly in some cases), but their original intent is different. The Factory makes more conventional but still weird toys and appliances, while Dr. Wondertainment makes things that are supposed to stand out and fascinate you.

Conclusion (but not really): Dr. Wondertainment is a brand The Factory uses on certain products. (Again, not really.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed, that's what got me to upvote. "So it makes you lose… oh. That's why."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's Scantron, not sorts.

Also, I'm glad I don't eat sweets much anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Burn in hell sorts. Burn in hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my eyes both brands' products are equal in lethality, or at least inherent danger. The only real difference is the Factory makes things for practicality, and Wondertainment makes things more aimed at entertainment, (Hence the names).

EDIT: Forgot to say, the fact that this can be worked in an entertaining fashion, but was made for practical use in the bath is the reason it's on the fence for so many people. It's like how you can use tin foil for decoration just as much as storage and wrapping.

I can't make a sure choice for either side, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "can be dangerous" is ok for wondertainment. After all, where would be the fun without risks? But a product that is actively harmful to the user even when used as directed? That just feels off to me, all I'm saying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not everything the Factory does is lethal. scp-248 scp-585 scp-644 scp-720 scp-721

Dr Wondertainment makes stuff that's fun (for a skewed value of "fun"). The Factory makes stuff you'd find in the Dr. Who's version of a Dollar store.

It's why I'd think SCP-161 would actually Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A question for those who think it would be better as a factory product - why? In terms of harmful effects, SCP-846, SCP-549, SCP-958 and to some extent SCP-445 are all Wondertainment, but they can still be dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As an aside, the thing that really makes it for me, asides from the picture is the negative nutritional value listed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though… Factory items seem to be much more purposeful… well, in theory anyways. Lot of stuff that Wondertainment makes can be dangerous.. say Super Paper…. this really seems like practical joke candy or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted and I agree - this is rather Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"What the hell are you meant to be?"
"I'm the bubblebath monster. Are We Cool Yet?"
"You're the wha-OH GOD"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be my main defense for it being a Dr. Wondertainment product - In my opinion it does meet the aim of amazing people in a disturbing fashion, it's just when you don't follow the instructions that it becomes lethal. Although I can definitely see why it would be fitting as a Factory item, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weeell, it's not harmful if you follow the directions.

Man, I want these for next halloween.
How awesome would that be?
You just walk into the party, dressed normally.
"What the hell are you meant to be?"
"I'm the bubblebath monster"
"You're the wha-OH GOD"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: agreed. wondertainment generally tries to amaze people in a disturbing fashion, they dont really seem to go for products that have direct physical effects upon their users. but the factory makes all kinds of potentially harmful stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, because I like the execution, but this sounds more like a Factory item then wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, if enough people eat enough pieces, there could be so much blood that it fills up an elevator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this should be Wondertainment or not, but holy crap the blood oh god oh god

I mean, it was a pretty neat little article.

item.title: Re: Hmm... Downvoted.
item.content:encoded: The warning label makes it make much more sense. Thanks.

item.title: Re: Hmm... Downvoted.
item.content:encoded: I had thought to put a warning label on it early on, but it must have slipped my mind at some point and I forgot to add it in. This has been amended now.

item.title: Re: Hmm... Downvoted.
item.content:encoded: It's only lethal if you eat more than one at a time. Otherwise, it just makes you exude pink foam. Which is very Wondertainment. Besides, 1373 is probably lethal. As is 846.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this SCP is not half bad, this is so ridiculously out of character for Wondertainment I downvoted it hard. Do not expect this to be in my personal canon of wondertainment items. My suggestion would be to take out the wondertainment references and have them just be bath beads made by an unknown or redacted organization. The wondertainment adds nothing to the article and is mainly detrimental to its suspension of disbelief.

item.title: Hmm... Downvoted.
item.content:encoded: This is the only Wondertainment thing I can think of that's immediately and maliciously lethal to the customer. Downvoted.
